questions.q: What is the primary purpose of a load balancer in cloud networking?
options: To encrypt all traffic between instances
To distribute incoming traffic across multiple backend instances for availability and performance
To prevent users from accessing certain instances
To cache static content from web servers
questions.explanation: Load balancers distribute incoming requests across multiple backend instances, improving availability, performance, and fault tolerance. Option 0 is encryption; option 2 describes security groups; option 3 describes CDNs.

questions.q: What is a security group in AWS?
options: A group of users with similar access permissions
A virtual firewall that controls inbound and outbound traffic to EC2 instances
A collection of VPCs that share security policies
A service that monitors suspicious network activity
questions.explanation: A security group is a stateful firewall controlling which traffic is allowed to reach instances. Option 0 is IAM; option 2 is not a real construct; option 3 describes monitoring services like VPC Flow Logs.

questions.q: What is the difference between a security group and a network ACL?
options: Security groups work at layer 3; network ACLs work at layer 4
Network ACLs are stateful; security groups are stateless
Security groups are instance-level stateful firewalls; network ACLs are subnet-level stateless firewalls
Network ACLs can only block traffic; security groups can allow and block
questions.explanation: Security groups are stateful and attached to instances; network ACLs (NACLs) are stateless and attached to subnets. Option 0 reverses the layer context; option 1 is reversed (security groups are stateful); option 3 is false (both allow and block).

questions.q: What is a subnet in the context of VPC networking?
options: A smaller VPC within a larger VPC
A range of IP addresses within a VPC, associated with a specific Availability Zone
A security rule that filters traffic at the protocol level
A tunnel that encrypts data between two VPCs
questions.explanation: A subnet is a segment of a VPC's IP address range, scoped to a single AZ, where instances are launched. Option 0 is incorrect nesting; option 2 is security groups; option 3 is VPC peering or VPN.

questions.q: What is DNS (Domain Name System) used for in cloud networks?
options: To encrypt data in transit between services
To translate human-readable domain names to IP addresses so users can access services by name
To distribute load across multiple instances
To authenticate users accessing cloud resources
questions.explanation: DNS maps domain names (example.com) to IP addresses, enabling human-friendly access to services. Option 0 is encryption (TLS); option 2 is load balancing; option 3 is authentication/IAM.

questions.q: What is the role of an Internet Gateway in a VPC?
options: To block all traffic from the internet to instances
To connect instances to on-premises data centers
To enable instances in public subnets to communicate with the internet
To prevent DDoS attacks from reaching instances
questions.explanation: An Internet Gateway allows instances in a VPC to communicate bidirectionally with the public internet. Option 0 is the opposite; option 1 describes a Virtual Private Gateway; option 3 describes DDoS protection services.

questions.q: What is a NAT Gateway used for in cloud networking?
options: To provide a public IP address to every instance in a VPC
To allow instances in private subnets to initiate outbound traffic to the internet while remaining unreachable from the internet
To route traffic between multiple VPCs
To encrypt all traffic leaving the VPC
questions.explanation: A NAT Gateway lets private instances make outbound connections to the internet (for updates, API calls) while staying protected from inbound connections. Option 0 is Internet Gateway; option 2 is peering/routing; option 3 is encryption.

questions.q: What types of load balancers are commonly used in cloud environments?
options: Only Layer 4 load balancers exist in the cloud
Application Load Balancer (Layer 7), Network Load Balancer (Layer 4), and Classic Load Balancer
Load balancers that only work with static IP addresses
Only software-based load balancers deployed on EC2 instances
questions.explanation: Cloud providers offer multiple load balancer types optimized for different use cases: ALB for HTTP/HTTPS, NLB for extreme performance/UDP, CLB for legacy applications. Options 0, 2, and 3 are unnecessarily restrictive or false.

questions.q: What is VPC peering in AWS?
options: A way to monitor network traffic between instances
A connection that enables private communication between two VPCs as if they were part of the same network
A service that automatically scales VPC subnets
A backup mechanism for VPC configurations
questions.explanation: VPC peering creates a networking connection between two VPCs for private, non-internet traffic. Option 0 is monitoring; option 2 is autoscaling; option 3 is backup/recovery.

questions.q: Why would you use Route 53 or similar managed DNS services?
options: To replace security groups in controlling traffic
To provide DNS resolution, health checks, traffic routing policies, and failover management
To eliminate the need for load balancers
To encrypt all data in transit across the network
questions.explanation: Managed DNS services handle domain resolution, health monitoring, intelligent routing, and automatic failover to healthy endpoints. Option 0 is incorrect; option 2 is wrong (they complement each other); option 3 is encryption, not DNS.
